title: Margherita Fumero

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Italian actress and comedian

| Margherita Fumero | |
| --- | --- |
| Fumero in 2010 | |
| Born | (1947-10-16) 16 October 1947 (age 78)  [Turin](/wiki/Turin "Turin"), Italy |
| Occupation | Actress |
| Height | 1.58 m (5 ft 2 in) |

**Margherita Fumero** (born 16 October 1947) is an Italian actress and comedian.

## Life and career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Margherita_Fumero&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Life and career")]

Born in [Turin](/wiki/Turin "Turin"), Fumero became first known as a member of the stage company of [Erminio Macario](/wiki/Erminio_Macario "Erminio Macario"). She achieved a large popularity as the fictional wife of [Enrico Beruschi](/wiki/Enrico_Beruschi "Enrico Beruschi") in a series of television variety shows, including *[Drive In](/wiki/Drive_In_(variety_show) "Drive In (variety show)")* and *Sabato al circo*, and on stage.[[1]](#cite_note-attr-1)[[2]](#cite_note-Grasso-2)

Fumero also appeared in a number of films, often directed by [Bruno Corbucci](/wiki/Bruno_Corbucci "Bruno Corbucci").[[1]](#cite_note-attr-1) In 2012 Fumero released an autobiography, *Quattro chiacchiere con Margherita*.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Partial filmography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Margherita_Fumero&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Partial filmography")]

* *[Due sul pianerottolo](/wiki/Due_sul_pianerottolo "Due sul pianerottolo")* (1976)
* *[Little Italy](/wiki/Little_Italy_(1978_film) "Little Italy (1978 film)")* (1978)
* *[The Gang That Sold America](/wiki/The_Gang_That_Sold_America "The Gang That Sold America")* (1979)
* *[Cat and Dog](/wiki/Cat_and_Dog "Cat and Dog")* (1983)
* *[Classe di ferro](/wiki/Classe_di_ferro "Classe di ferro")* (TV, 1989-1991)
* *[Caméra Café](/wiki/Cam%C3%A9ra_Caf%C3%A9 "Caméra Café")* (TV, 2003)
